text: Dropping the guards didn’t make me better. It made it real.
drafts: Dropping the guards didn’t make me better. It made it real.||Left felt natural. Right felt possible. Neither felt settled.||Guards Down came from the moment protection stopped making the choice for me.

text: Older song, current mood: Brain Rot.
drafts: Older song, current mood: Brain Rot.||Brain Rot was a joke until it started sounding like a diary.||Some songs stay current because the weather inside your head never really clears.

text: Guards Down came from bowling with Da, trying to choose between left and right while the bumpers were finally down. Full signal on YouTube, and if you’re into this kind of transmission, subscribe while we build to 1,000.
drafts: Guards Down came from bowling with Da, trying to choose between left and right while the bumpers were finally down. Full signal on YouTube, and if you’re into this kind of transmission, subscribe while we build to 1,000.||This song started at a bowling lane: no bumpers, no clean answer, just trying to decide which hand was really mine. Watch Guards Down on YouTube.||Da was already talking scholarships. I was still trying to figure out left or right. That gap became Guards Down.
